key_insight: The paper turns mixed finite-element conservation laws into a Gaussian-process surrogate, giving structure-preserving PDE predictions with closed-form uncertainty estimates for fluxes and boundary quantities.
engineering_relevance: This enables near-real-time, verifiable surrogate simulation for PDE-driven engineering systems where both speed and trustworthy uncertainty quantification matter.
method: Use a lightweight transformer to select compatible H(div)–L2 reduced spaces from Raviart–Thomas and dgP0 elements.
Formulate training as an optimal recovery problem with conservation-law equality constraints and solve it via a fast Schur-complement strategy.
Model inter-volume fluxes with a Gaussian process to obtain a Dirichlet-to-Neumann map and analytic posterior uncertainty.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing accurate posterior distributions that can be used for error estimation, alongside RKHS posterior error bounds.
watch_item: The approach depends on careful construction of mixed FEM subspaces and constrained GP training, which may be sensitive to mesh design, problem class, and implementation complexity.
paper_title: Structure-Preserving Neural Surrogates with Tractable Uncertainty Quantification
